stat_of_week: 12 - North Correa stole 12 bases in a single week, tying the current season record set by themselves, while still losing the matchup 5-4 to the Benchwarmers — the most steals any team has posted all year couldn't prevent a defeat
thriller_teams: Wisconsin Rapids Reapers vs Buttville Skidmarks
thriller_note: The Reapers squeaked out a one-category win despite the Skidmarks posting a better team ERA, more innings, and a massive NSVH advantage of 8, but Davis Martin's 19 Ks and a league-best 1.71 ERA carried Wisconsin Rapids over the top in a matchup that could have easily gone either way — the Skidmarks remain frustratingly stuck in neutral despite real pitching talent.
key_storyline: Sugar Land Skeeters delivered the most dominant performance of Week 7, dismantling Canberra Wombats 9-1 in what amounts to a near-perfect week: 12 HR, 36 RBI, 35 runs, a 2.08 ERA, 6 quality starts, and 9 NSVH. Eduardo Rodriguez was virtually unhittable at 0.59 ERA over 15.1 innings, and the offense was firing on every cylinder from top to bottom. After slipping to the preseason favorite's slot following a rough Week 6 blowout loss, the Skeeters have sent a loud message to the rest of the league that last week was an aberration — and with a date against General Hospital looming in Week 8, the timing of this resurgence could not be better.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers carry the largest positive luck delta in the entire league at +1.62, and their 5-4 win over North Correa this week was propped up by a North Correa squad that posted 12 steals, 31 runs, and a better overall pitching line — the Benchwarmers survived on RBI production and a thin edge in power categories rather than any dominant performance.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs posted a league-competitive 73 strikeouts, 6 quality starts, a 3.45 ERA, and 32 RBI — genuinely strong marks in multiple categories — yet lost to Hiroshima Radiation 6-4, and their season-long luck delta of -0.89 is the worst in the league, suggesting this team is consistently delivering more value than their record reflects.
records_broken: North Correa tied the season record for most stolen bases in a week with 12 SB, matching the previous mark also set by North Correa — the record stands but the achievement is hollow given they still lost the matchup 5-4.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | A 9-1 demolition of Canberra with 12 HR, 36 RBI, 6 QS, and Eduardo Rodriguez at 0.59 ERA is the most complete week any team has had in weeks — the Skeeters are back and dangerous | up

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Bryce Harper's 4 HR and 7 runs, Schwarber's 5 HR, and a league-high 7 QS with a 2.58 ERA make this the most balanced roster in the league right now; the offense finally showed up alongside elite pitching | up

3. BaseballPro77 | Bobby Witt Jr.'s 3 HR and 7 runs, Bryce Elder's 1.54 ERA, and Logan Gilbert's 0.75 WHIP powered a 6-4 win over General Hospital; this team just won a measuring-stick game and looks like a genuine contender | same

4. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Davis Martin's 19 Ks and a team ERA of 1.71 — the new season record for lowest ERA by a winning team — are elite outputs; a 4-3 grind over Buttville with real pitching depth forming is a dangerous combination | up

5. Athletic Park Dragons | A 5-5 tie with the Bombers is a mild disappointment given Cristopher Sánchez's 0.67 WHIP and Aaron Judge's 3 HR, but the pitching staff remains the league's most improved and a QS total of 5 is a weekly anchor | down

6. Riverside Roughnecks | A convincing 7-3 win over the Twins backed by Seiya Suzuki, Xavier Edwards, and the massive waiver haul of Roki Sasaki, Bassitt, and Fedde signals real intent; this rotation could look completely different in two weeks | up

7. Albuquerque Bombers | A 5-5 tie when your pitching posts a 5.15 ERA and 1.47 WHIP is honestly a small miracle — Buxton's 3 HR, Naylor's 3 SB, and Turang's 2 HR kept it respectable, but the rotation is a serious concern heading into a Hiroshima matchup | down

8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | The 3-7 loss hides a decent offensive week from Ramírez and Wilson, but dropping to zero NSVH and posting only 48 IP is unsustainable; the aggressive waiver wire work adding Dollander and Montero is the right call | down

9. General Hospital | Andy Pages' 4 HR and 8 RBI and Kyle Tucker's .478 OBP are trade-value peaks, but losing 6-4 despite 10 HR, 32 RBI, and 6 NSVH to BaseballPro77 stings — a Week 8 showdown with the Skeeters is now a must-win situation | down

10. North Correa | Posting a league-record-tying 12 SB, 31 runs, and 2 solid starts while still losing 5-4 is the definition of this team's season — elite speed and respectable pitching undercut by a total lack of power; 3 HR all week is unworkable | same

11. Benchwarmers | Cody Bellinger's 8 RBI and Dubón's 5 RBI are nice, but this team's +1.62 luck delta is an unsustainable house of cards; zero NSVH and a rotation with only 2 QS means the underlying talent cannot support their record long-term | same

12. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Pete Alonso's 7 RBI, 6 QS, and 73 Ks are legitimate — this team is not as bad as their record suggests, and the -0.89 luck delta is a real injustice; Tyler Mahle adds rotation depth but they need the schedule to even out | up

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Eight NSVH and a 3.53 ERA are genuinely good pitching marks but losing 4-3 to a Reapers team with fewer innings and lower power output is a familiar story — this roster cannot score enough runs to win close matchups | same

14. Canberra Wombats | Getting blown out 9-1 with a .254 OBP and only 14 runs scored while dropping Jake Bennett off the roster is moving in the wrong direction on every front; deGrom's 4.05 ERA is concerning and the offense is the worst in the league this week | down
waiver_highlights: Riverside Roughnecks | A | Added Roki Sasaki, Chris Bassitt, Erick Fedde, Travis Bazzana; dropped four relievers and a utility piece | This is a transformational haul — Sasaki alone is a top-50 SP add if healthy, Bassitt is a reliable innings-eater, and Fedde gives rotation depth; pairing this with an already-improving pitching staff makes the Roughnecks a genuine sleeper threat.

Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | B | Added Daniel Lynch IV, Braydon Fisher, Kevin Kelly, Brent Headrick; dropped Drew Pomeranz | Loading up on bullpen arms after a 1.71 ERA week suggests the manager is looking to lock in the NSVH and ERA categories as identity stats; quality of the adds is modest but the volume and timing make sense.

Hiroshima Radiation | B | Added Trevor McDonald, George Soriano, Gus Varland, Kyle Karros; dropped Ryan Walker, Carlos Correa | McDonald immediately contributed with a 1.29 ERA and 0.29 WHIP in 7 IP on debut, validating the add; shedding Carlos Correa is a bold call that signals a pivot away from name value toward active contributors.

Abbotsford-Colby Twins | B | Added Chase Dollander, Keider Montero, Chase Meidroth; dropped Grant Holmes, Andrew Painter, Caleb Durbin | Dollander is the headliner and a high-upside SP add with front-of-rotation potential; the aggressive rebuild of the pitching staff after a rough week shows urgency, though Montero is a deeper stash play.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | C | Added Tyler Mahle, Matthew Liberatore, Ian Seymour; dropped Justin Bruihl, Cam Smith (and oddly re-added then dropped Liberatore) | Mahle is a legitimate rotation piece if he's returning to form, but the Liberatore ping-pong and adding Seymour suggest some roster indecision; the team needs bats more urgently than pitching depth.
